onboarding-details:
We need to set and confirm the city where you want to do the inventory.
You are one step closer to getting your GPC emissions inventory!
setup-city-details: Search and select the city or municipality you'll work with.
ai-extract-no-rows: AI could not extract inventory rows from this table. The file may use column names we don't recognize—try a file with columns like Sector, Sub-sector, Scope, and emissions (e.g. total CO2e), or export from your source in a standard eCRF/CIRIS layout.
inventory-not-found-for-target-year: Inventory not found for the target year
file-year-mismatch-title: File year does not match inventory
file-year-mismatch: The file's data year does not match the inventory target year. Please upload a file with data for {{year}}.
import-completed-description: Your inventory data has been imported successfully.
breaking-into-chunks: Breaking file into smaller chunks for easy extraction
interpreting-file-description: AI is mapping columns and extracting inventory rows. This may take a moment.
setup-inventory-details-description: Provide some basic details about the inventory you want to create. You can edit these settings afterwards.
inventory-goal-description: Want to learn more about these inventory formats? <2>Learn more</2> about the GPC Framework.
gwp-description: If this is your first inventory, we recommend using AR6 (latest version) for your inventory calculations. If your city has previous inventories, use the same version as before. <2>Learn more</2> about GWP.
setup-population-data-description: Provide the population data for your country, region, and city. For accurate results, use values from the year closest to your inventory year. We use open data sources to pre-fill the information below.
gpc-basic-message: Only GPC Basic Inventories are supported momentarily. For population values the closest year to the city inventory is recommended, as it will be used for calculations and included in your GPC report.
confirm-description: Review the data for your city before proceeding.
confirm-inventory-description: Review the data for your city's inventory before proceeding.
confirm-details:
Review and confirm this information about your city. If there is an error please send us an email to edit it.

We use <2>open data sources to pre-fill the city profile.
done-heading: Your GHG emission inventory is ready to start!
done-description: Great job! You can review, add and update your inventory data anytime. Start completing your GHG emissions inventory now!
done-details: You created your city profile to start your GPC Basic GHG inventory.
information-required: This information is required and affects your GHG inventory accuracy.
population-required: Please enter value & year for population
inventory-creation-description: In this step, configure your city's GHG emissions inventory by selecting the inventory year, setting the target, and adding contextual data such as population.
city-boundary-info: In case the geographical boundary is not the right one <0><1>Contact Us</1></0>
unselected-city-boundary-heading: Search and select the city to be shown on the map
unselected-city-boundary-description: You will be able to check the geographical boundary for your inventory
welcome-title: Let's start with information about your city
welcome-description: In this step, choose a city to include in your project and provide relevant details that wil be used later.
city-population-required: City population and year are required
region-population-required: Region population and year are required
country-population-required: Country population and year are required
city-name-locode-required: City name and location code are required
upload-inventory-file-description: Upload your existing GHG inventory file. We support CSV, XLSX, and PDF (for AI extraction).
click-to-upload: Click to upload or drag and drop
validation-results-description: A key-value format with {{count}} fields has been detected. Review the interpretations below.
validation-results-description-pdf: {{count}} rows were extracted from the PDF. Review the data below.
map-rows-description: Review and adjust the mapping of your data to the inventory fields.
map-rows-description-pdf: Extracted rows are already mapped to inventory fields. Review the data below and continue to the final step.
review-confirm-description: Review the import summary before finalizing.
data-loss-warning-message: You have unsaved changes. If you leave this page, your progress will be lost. Are you sure you want to continue?
inventory-goal-description-ghgi: The GPC provides a robust framework for accounting and reporting city-wide greenhouse gas emissions.
gwp-description-ghgi: Global warming potential (GWP) is a measure of how much heat a greenhouse gas traps in the atmosphere over a specific time period, relative to carbon dioxide.
gwp-description-ghgi-tip: We recommend using AR6 (latest version) for your inventory calculations. If your city has previous inventories, use the same version as before.
drawer-help-section-description: In this section you'll find information related to the terms on this screen.
gpc-definition-description: The Global Protocol for Community-Scale Greenhouse Gas Emission Inventories (GPC) is a set of guidelines that helps cities and local communities measure and report their greenhouse gas emissions in a clear, consistent way.
gpc-definition-bullet-point-1: It's like a common rulebook for cities around the world.
gpc-definition-bullet-point-2: It tells them how to count the pollution they produce, like from cars, buildings, and waste.
gpc-definition-bullet-point-3: This helps cities understand their impact on climate change.
gpc-definition-bullet-point-4: With this information, they can make better plans to reduce emissions and protect the environment.
gpc-definition-summary: The GPC makes sure everyone measures emissions the same way, so cities can compare progress and work together to fight climate change
gpc-basic-definition-description: GPC Basic is a simplified version of the Global Protocol for Community-Scale Greenhouse Gas Emission Inventories (GPC)
gpc-basic-definition-bullet-point-1: GPC Basic focuses on the most important and easiest-to-measure emissions, like those from energy use in buildings and transportation
gpc-basic-definition-bullet-point-2: It helps communities take the first step in understanding and managing their climate impact without getting overwhelmed
gpc-basic-plus-definition-description: GPC Basic Plus is a step up from GPC Basic in the Global Protocol for Community-Scale Greenhouse Gas Emission Inventories
gpc-basic-plus-definition-bullet-point-1: It's for cities or communities that already have some experience measuring emissions and want to include more detailed sources.
gpc-basic-plus-definition-bullet-point-2: GPC Basic Plus adds extra categories like waste, water, and some industrial emissions beyond just energy and transport.
gpc-basic-plus-definition-bullet-point-3: It helps communities get a fuller picture of their greenhouse gas emissions to improve their climate actions
gpc-basic-plus-definition-summary: So, it's like moving from a beginner's level to an intermediate level in tracking emissions, making the data more complete and useful.
gwp-ar5-definition-description: The Fifth Assessment Report (AR5) of the United Nations Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) is a major scientific report published in 2014.
gwp-ar5-definition-bullet-point-1: It summarizes the latest and most reliable science about climate change.
gwp-ar5-definition-bullet-point-2: It explains how human activities are changing the climate, what impacts are happening, and what might happen in the future.
gwp-ar5-definition-bullet-point-3: It also looks at ways to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and adapt to changes.
gwp-ar5-definition-bullet-point-4: Governments and policymakers use this report to make informed decisions to protect the planet.
gwp-ar5-definition-summary: Think of AR5 as a big, trusted climate science update that helps the world understand and respond to climate change.
gwp-ar6-definition-description: The AR6 is the Sixth Assessment Report from the United Nations Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC), released starting in 2021.
gwp-ar6-definition-bullet-point-1: It's the newest, most up-to-date big report on climate science.
gwp-ar6-definition-bullet-point-2: It shows the latest findings about how the climate is changing, the impacts we're already seeing, and what could happen if we act-or don't.
gwp-ar6-definition-bullet-point-3: It also highlights ways to reduce emissions and adapt to climate change.
gwp-ar6-definition-bullet-point-4: AR6 helps governments and people make better decisions to protect the planet's future.
gwp-ar6-definition-summary: Think of AR6 as the latest and clearest climate science guide to help us understand and tackle climate change today
